name: SpaceX Recent Launches Research
description: Find the 3 most recent SpaceX launches and save mission details to a file

tasks.name: Search for recent SpaceX launches
tasks.description: You MUST call web_search with the query 'SpaceX launches 2026 recent missions'. Output the FULL raw search results verbatim (URLs, titles, snippets). Do NOT summarize as 'results were found' — downstream tasks need the actual data. Include at least 5-10 search results with complete snippets that mention launch dates, mission names, and outcomes.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains at least 3 URLs starting with https:// and snippets mentioning SpaceX launch dates or mission names from 2025-2026

tasks.name: Extract launch details
tasks.description: From task_001's search results, identify the 3 MOST RECENT SpaceX launches by date. For each launch extract: (1) mission name, (2) exact launch date, (3) success status (successful/failed/partial). Do NOT invent launch details. If a field is unclear from search results, write 'not found'. Sort by date descending (newest first). Output format: numbered list with 'Mission: <name>, Date: <date>, Status: <status>' for each of the 3 launches. Cite which search result snippet each launch came from.
tasks.success_criteria: Output contains exactly 3 launches, each with Mission, Date, and Status fields filled in, sorted by date with newest first

tasks.name: Write results to file
tasks.description: You MUST call file_write to save the launch information from task_002 to a file named 'spacex_launches.txt'. Use the exact content from task_002's output without modification. The file_path parameter must be 'spacex_launches.txt'.
tasks.success_criteria: Output confirms file_write was called successfully with path 'spacex_launches.txt' and content from task_002
